when_to_use.description: Use Schedule A if itemized deductions exceed the standard deduction
common_reasons_to_itemize: Significant state and local taxes (up to $10,000 cap)
Major medical expenses exceeding 7.5% of AGI

f1_04.description: Subtract line 3 from line 1. If zero or less, enter 0

f1_05.description: State and local income taxes OR general sales taxes

c1_01.description: Check if using sales tax instead of income tax

f1_06.description: State and local real estate taxes

f1_07.description: State and local personal property taxes

f1_09.description: Enter the smaller of line 5d or $10,000 ($5,000 if MFS)

f1_12.description: Home mortgage interest and points from Form 1098

f1_13.description: Home mortgage interest not reported on Form 1098

f1_20.description: Other than by cash or check (attach Form 8283 if over $500)

f1_23.description: Casualty and theft loss(es) from federally declared disaster (attach Form 4684)

f1_24.description: Other itemized deductions (list type and amount)

f1_26.description: If you elect to itemize, enter amount from line 17 here and on Form 1040, line 12

calculation_rules.line_17: Line 4 + Line 7 + Line 10 + Line 14 + Line 15 + Line 16

salt_cap.description: State and local tax (SALT) deduction is capped at $10,000 ($5,000 if MFS)

mortgage_interest.description: Interest on mortgage debt up to $750,000 ($1M for pre-Dec 2017 debt)

charitable.description: Cash contributions limited to 60% of AGI; appreciated property to 30%

medical.description: Only medical expenses exceeding 7.5% of AGI are deductible

common_mistakes: Including taxes paid for prior years without adjustment
Not having documentation for charitable contributions over $250
Deducting medical expenses paid with HSA/FSA funds
Including mortgage interest on debt exceeding the limit
